title: spawn_blocking for blocking wolfhsm client in async context is correct
description: wolfhsm::Client is Send but not Sync and all methods take &mut self (blocking). Wrapping all HSM calls in tokio::task::spawn_blocking is the correct async-Rust pattern: the blocking work runs on the blocking thread pool, the async executor is not stalled. The Arc<Mutex<WolfHsmState>> provides the needed interior mutability. A contributor might suggest tokio::sync::Mutex (async-aware), but std::sync::Mutex inside spawn_blocking is correct and cheaper — the mutex is always locked and released within a single synchronous call, never across an await point.
